trevor-bachmeyer.acute_vs_chronic_note: Dr Bachmeyer often distinguishes acute (injury/infection) vs chronic (longevity/maintenance) dosing. Acute_signals + chronic_signals fields surface candidate contexts; full distillation pending Codex pass.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider Dihexa's 12-13 day half-life as the operative gating factor — Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "Half-life: 12-13 days. Extraordinarily long" and Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:49:01.480): "the Dihexa half-life is like 12 days" both push 2-3x per week, not daily. We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh sequencing because Trevor (hj0jqdfsg3o 00:18:30.880): "here's when dihexa slides into this mix. After if you fuel with MS C right handle the mitochondria after you repair with the cerebral after you calm the system" — synaptogenesis on an inflamed substrate is, per Trevor (hj0jqdfsg3o 00:18:21.679): "expending energy at your expense in an inflammatory environment and wondering why you feel worse." For dose, Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24) lists the oral range "5 to 40 mg" with "Hunter's sweet spot: 10 mg" and Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:49:11.720): "I would start at like 5 to 10 milligrams."
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Sleep impact at high cumulative dosing: Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:49:14.880): "It can affect your sleep. That's why I That's why I say you don't want to use Dihexa every day" and (JC1poI1OEog 00:49:19.920): "If there's a cumulative use of it every single day for several weeks, you can kind of get a build up where some people will have their sleep affected."
- Don't use as a standalone nootropic shortcut: Trevor (hj0jqdfsg3o 00:18:13.120): "You can't just dump it into the mix and go synaptogenesis. Let's just build all the neurons we can that are going nowhere doing having no function expending energy at your expense in an inflammatory environment."
- Sequencing matters: Trevor (hj0jqdfsg3o 00:19:17.840): "That's how DHEXA is meant to be used. So throwing it together and putting it in with something else and wrapping it in a little package and going, 'This is going to be the next Limitless pill makes me want to slap somebody in the mouth.'"
- Cycle, don't run forever: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "Cycling: 8-12 weeks ON, 2-4 weeks OFF" and "Off-day support: Alpha-GPC to replenish neurotransmitters."
- Discipline floor: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "Start low. Work up. Cycle responsibly. Don't jump to 40 mg/day because someone on the internet said so."
- Pregnancy / seizure / severe psych contraindications surfaced for the BioMind dihexa-containing stack: Hunter email threadId 1981023143d4996d (2025-07-15): "Who should avoid it? Pregnant/nursing women (not studied); Those with active seizure disorders or severe psychiatric illness; If you're on prescription meds, check with your doctor (especially blood thinners, SSRIs, or antipsychotics)."
- No clinical trial ceiling published: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "Will we ever have extensive clinical data on Dihexa itself? Probably not. No pharmaceutical company is going to invest hundreds of millions to take a simple peptide through FDA approval when they can't patent it."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: Dihexa is a synaptogenesis-driving nootropic worth using, with a 12-13 day half-life that mandates non-daily dosing. Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "Half-life: 12-13 days. Extraordinarily long. Implications: Cumulative effect when dosing regularly; Don't need daily dosing — 2-3x/week is plenty." Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:49:01.480): "the Dihexa half-life is like 12 days" and (JC1poI1OEog 00:49:06.800): "I would do three times a week tops if you were doing Dihexa." Trevor (hj0jqdfsg3o 00:19:43.120): "It's going to be one of the top three I think anyone should take. Excuse me. That's how good it is." Mechanism / use-case agreement: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "Promotes synaptogenesis = forms NEW synaptic connections between neurons" and Trevor (hj0jqdfsg3o 00:18:59.840): "DHEXA becomes the construction crew that comes in and builds all of the new highways and infrastructure on ground that is solid." Cognitive-edge framing vs Modafinil also overlaps: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24) lists "Healthy cognitive edge (vs Modafinil) — Great fit — clean processing power, no personality stripping" and Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:48:46.359): "To me, it it's way less mild than something like modafinil."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Dose ceiling and route. Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24) gives an oral range "5 to 40 mg" with "Hunter's sweet spot: 10 mg" and an explicit injectable lane "Subcutaneous injection — Dose: 1-3 mg, 2-3x per week — Bioavailability: 100% (vs 38% oral)". Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:49:11.720) is narrower oral-only: "I would start at like 5 to 10 milligrams" with no injectable mention in the same conversation. Trevor (hj0jqdfsg3o) gives no specific Dihexa milligrams in the cited episode — instead frames Dihexa as part of a sequenced stack and warns against using it without first treating inflammation; he gives Dihexa a conceptual role ("construction crew") rather than a numeric protocol, so Hunter's numbers and Taylor's numbers stand without a Trevor counter-number. Frequency framing also splits: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24) writes "Frequency: 3x per week, or just on days needing deep work" while Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:49:06.800): "I would do three times a week tops" but cautions "do whatever you want" (JC1poI1OEog 00:49:10.480). Stack-context: Hunter pairs Dihexa as one of three actives in BioMind — Hunter email threadId 1981023143d4996d (2025-07-15): "One capsule of BioMind contains: 10mg J-147; 10mg Dihexa; 10mg Noopept" — whereas Trevor (hj0jqdfsg3o 00:18:05.760) treats it as terminal-stage only: "Dhexa, it's useful. So, believe me, I'll put it in here, but you have to know what you're doing with it. You can use it after."

acute.dose: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "Range: 5 to 40 mg"; "Hunter's sweet spot: 10 mg"; "5 mg = subtle"; "10 mg = solid cognitive bump without being pushed too far." Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:49:11.720): "I would start at like 5 to 10 milligrams."
acute.route: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "Oral capsules (most common route)" with "Bioavailability: ~38% oral"; alternative "Subcutaneous injection — Dose: 1-3 mg, 2-3x per week — Bioavailability: 100% (vs 38% oral)."
acute.frequency: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "Frequency: 3x per week, or just on days needing deep work."
acute.duration: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "Take oral capsules that day" — i.e., single-day deep-work pulse with no ramp.
acute.verbatim_citation: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "Deep work focus days — Take oral capsules that day." Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:48:50.560): "that you can use on a two to three a day a week basis. Yeah, you don't want to overuse that."

chronic.dose: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "Hunter's sweet spot: 10 mg" oral or "Subcutaneous injection — Dose: 1-3 mg, 2-3x per week." Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:49:11.720): "I would start at like 5 to 10 milligrams" with sleep-driven cap.
chronic.route: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): oral 38% bioavailability vs subq 100% — "Crosses BBB orally — most neurotrophic factors can't."
chronic.frequency: Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "3x per week, or just on days needing deep work"; Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:49:06.800): "I would do three times a week tops if you were doing Dihexa"; both say not daily because Hunter email threadId 19d21e15f89c4e2f (2026-03-24): "Half-life: 12-13 days. Extraordinarily long" and Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:49:14.880): "It can affect your sleep. That's why I That's why I say you don't want to use Dihexa every day."
